pattern: A fixed-size letter-frequency count for each string reduces the whole comparison to checking, for every letter, that the two counts never differ by more than the allowed slack.
hints: Count how many times each of the 26 letters appears in each string.
For every letter, compare its count in the two strings; the strings are almost equivalent only if none of those differences exceeds the allowed amount.
Letters that appear in one string but not the other still need to be compared -- their count on the missing side is simply 0.

edge_inputs.description: one letter differs by exactly one more than allowed
identical strings, every difference is zero
single differing characters, well within the allowance
every differing letter sits exactly at the allowed threshold
a single letter exceeds the threshold by one
an anagram of the other string, every difference is zero despite different order

stress.description: random equal-length lowercase strings at the max length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    w1 = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    w2 = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [w1, w2]
